title: sizeof(sizeof(...)) should be avoided
description: "sizeof(sizeof(...))" should not be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoGiven an expressioneof typeT,sizeof(e)returns the size in bytes ofT. Thesizeofoperator results in a value of typesize_t. Also,sizeof(e)has no side effects becauseeis not evaluated.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
int array[10];
size_t wrong_size = sizeof(sizeof(array));  // Noncompliant: nested sizeof

struct Point { int x, y; };
size_t value = sizeof(sizeof(struct Point));  // Noncompliant
compliant:
int array[10];
size_t array_size = sizeof(array);  // Compliant: single sizeof

struct Point { int x, y; };
size_t point_size = sizeof(struct Point);  // Compliant

// If you need size of size_t type
size_t size_of_sizet = sizeof(size_t);  // Compliant

checks.message: Sizeof(sizeof(...)) should be avoided. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
